cycle.message: graph contains a cycle through node "{nodeId}"

param-uniform-type-conflict.message: param uniform "{name}" is declared as both {priorType} and {newType}

bad-structural-param-value.message: param "{key}" must be one of {allowed}; got {got}

structural-param-immutable.message: structural param "{key}" of a manual node cannot change under a stable id - recreate the node with a new id

unknown-render-stage.message: node "{nodeId}" declares unknown stage "{stage}"; render nodes must be "vertex" or "fragment"

stage-input-mismatch.message: input "{socketKey}" of {consumerStage} node "{consumerNodeId}" reads a {producerStage}-stage value from node "{producerNodeId}"; a {producerStage} value cannot flow into the {consumerStage} stage

stage-output-mismatch.message: output slot "{slot}" is filled in the {slotStage} stage but node "{nodeId}" produces its value in the {producerStage} stage

opaque-type-crosses-stage.message: opaque type {typeId} cannot cross shader stages through a varying

varying-from-fragment-stage.message: node "{nodeId}" allocated a varying from the fragment stage; varyings are produced in the vertex stage

output-not-produced.message: node "{nodeId}" is bound to output slot "{slot}" but produced no value for socket "{socketKey}"

bad-finite-scalar-or-vector.message: FXNodeDefinition: {where} expects a finite number or a 1-4 component vector

bad-curve-point.message: FXNodeDefinition: {where} expects curve points of finite { position, value } numbers

bad-curve-interpolation.message: FXNodeDefinition: {where} curve point interpolation must be "smooth" or "sharp"

empty-gradient.message: FXNodeDefinition: {where} expects at least one gradient stop

bad-gradient-stop.message: FXNodeDefinition: {where} expects stops of { position: number, color: [r,g,b,a] }

bad-target-buffer-identifier.message: target "{targetName}" buffer "{bufferName}" is not a valid identifier (it is spliced into the generated kernel source)

duplicate-target-buffer.message: target "{targetName}" declares buffer "{bufferName}" more than once

bad-target-buffer-stride.message: target "{targetName}" buffer "{bufferName}" stride {stride} must be a positive integer

undeclared-target-buffer.message: target "{targetName}" {label} names buffer "{buffer}", which is not declared

bad-target-offset.message: target "{targetName}" {label} offset {offset} must be an integer

bad-target-input-identifier.message: target "{targetName}" input "{inputName}" is not a valid identifier

duplicate-target-input.message: target "{targetName}" declares input "{inputName}" more than once

spawn-input-has-dt.message: target "{targetName}" declares input "dt", but a spawn kernel has no dt

dt-input-has-offsets.message: target "{targetName}" declares input "dt" with offsets; "dt" is the synthesized timestep and must be offset-less

target-input-offset-count-mismatch.message: target "{targetName}" input "{inputName}" has {offsetCount} offset(s) but its {typeId} type needs {typeComponents}

offsetless-input-not-reserved.message: target "{targetName}" input "{inputName}" declares no offsets; offset-less inputs are reserved for "dt", "PARTICLE_INDEX", and the model matrix

duplicate-target-output.message: target "{targetName}" declares output slot "{outputSlot}" more than once

target-output-offset-count-mismatch.message: target "{targetName}" output "{outputSlot}" has {offsetCount} offset(s) but its {typeId} type needs {typeComponents}

duplicate-output-offset-write.message: target "{targetName}" output "{outputSlot}" writes offset {offset} more than once (a component would be silently overwritten)

duplicate-integration-offset-write.message: target "{targetName}" integration writes offset {offset} in more than one step (a later step silently overwrites an earlier one)

integration-ref-not-target-input.message: target "{targetName}" integration contains a "{refKind}" ref ("{refName}"); integration expressions may only read target inputs

integration-ref-not-scalar.message: target "{targetName}" integration reads input "{refName}" as a {typeId}; integration refs must be scalar

integration-reads-dt-in-spawn.message: target "{targetName}" integration reads "dt", but a spawn kernel has no dt

integration-input-not-declared.message: target "{targetName}" integration reads input "{refName}", which is not declared

phase-buffer-not-in-update.message: spawn target "{spawnName}" declares buffer "{bufferName}", which the update target "{updateName}" does not declare (the host allocates buffers from the update target only)

phase-buffer-stride-mismatch.message: buffer "{bufferName}" has stride {spawnStride} in spawn target "{spawnName}" but stride {updateStride} in update target "{updateName}"

bad-behavior-targets-shape.message: behavior targets must be an object

unresolvable-target-input.message: FXKernelContext.readTargetInput: builtin "{name}" is not a resolvable vector

unknown-target-value-type.message: target "{targetName}" {label} has an unknown value type "{glslTypeName}" ({components} component(s)); use the canonical FX_VALUE_TYPES entries

bad-render-input-identifier.message: target "{targetName}" input "{inputName}" is not a valid GLSL identifier

render-input-is-glsl-keyword.message: target "{targetName}" input "{inputName}" is a GLSL keyword

render-input-matches-generated-pattern.message: target "{targetName}" input "{inputName}" matches the compiler-generated "u_/v_..._<digits>" identifier form, which is reserved

ir-matrix-mul-dimension-mismatch.message: sparcoon IR: mul of matrices needs equal dimensions, got {aType} and {bType}

ir-matrix-vector-mul-width-mismatch.message: sparcoon IR: mul {aType} * {bType}: matrix dimension must match the vector width

ir-vector-matrix-mul-width-mismatch.message: sparcoon IR: mul {aType} * {bType}: vector width must match the matrix dimension

ir-matrix-mul-unsupported.message: sparcoon IR: mul is not defined for {aType} and {bType}

ir-matrix-add-sub-mismatch.message: sparcoon IR: {op} of matrices needs two equal matrix types, got {aType} and {bType}

ir-matrix-op-unsupported.message: sparcoon IR: {op} is not defined for {aType} and {bType}

ir-arithmetic-bad-operand-type.message: sparcoon IR: {op} expects float/vecN operands, got {aType} and {bType}

ir-mod-int-unsupported.message: sparcoon IR: mod is not defined for int/ivecN (GLSL's mod() has no integer overload)

ir-arithmetic-no-left-splat.message: sparcoon IR: {op} does not splat a scalar left operand against {bType}

ir-comparison-bad-operand-type.message: sparcoon IR: comparison {op} requires float operands, got {aType} and {bType}

ir-int-literal-not-integer.message: sparcoon IR: litInt expects an integer value, got {value}

ir-vector-literal-bad-width.message: sparcoon IR: litVec needs 2..4 components, got {count}

ir-negate-bad-operand.message: sparcoon IR: neg expects a float/vecN operand, got {typeId}

ir-no-matching-overload.message: sparcoon IR: no signature of "{name}" accepts ({argumentTypes})

ir-swizzle-bad-channel-count.message: sparcoon IR: swizzle needs 1..4 channels, got "{channels}"

ir-swizzle-bad-source.message: sparcoon IR: swizzle needs a vec2/vec3/vec4 source, got {typeId}

ir-swizzle-unknown-channel.message: sparcoon IR: invalid swizzle channel "{channel}" (expected x/y/z/w)

ir-swizzle-channel-out-of-range.message: sparcoon IR: swizzle channel "{channel}" out of range for {typeId}

ir-column-bad-source.message: sparcoon IR: column needs a mat2/mat3/mat4 source, got {typeId}

ir-column-index-out-of-range.message: sparcoon IR: column index {index} out of range for {typeId} (0..{maxIndex})

ir-construct-bad-target-type.message: sparcoon IR: construct target must be vec2/vec3/vec4, got {typeId}

ir-construct-bad-argument-type.message: sparcoon IR: construct argument must be float/vecN, got {typeId}

ir-construct-component-count-mismatch.message: sparcoon IR: construct({typeId}) needs {expected} components, got {got}

ir-construct-matrix-bad-form.message: sparcoon IR: construct({typeId}) needs {dimension} vec{dimension} columns, {scalarCount} floats, or a single matrix to resize

ir-no-implicit-int-float-conversion.message: sparcoon IR: cannot implicitly convert {fromType} to {targetType} - use an explicit int/float cast

ir-to-int-bad-operand.message: sparcoon IR: toInt expects a float operand, got {typeId}

ir-to-float-bad-operand.message: sparcoon IR: toFloat expects an int operand, got {typeId}

ir-select-condition-not-float.message: sparcoon IR: select condition must be float, got {typeId}

ir-select-branch-type-mismatch.message: sparcoon IR: select branches must share a type, got {aType} and {bType}

node-factory-type-mismatch.message: FXNodeRegistry: factory for "{requestedType}" produced a node of type "{producedType}"

glsl-float-not-finite.message: sparcoon IR: non-finite number {value} cannot be printed as a GLSL literal

glsl-int-not-finite.message: sparcoon IR: {value} cannot be printed as a GLSL int literal (not a finite integer)
